channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harris viewed more positively by Hispanic women than by Hispanic men: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A solid majority of Hispanic women have a positive opinion of Vice President Kamala Harris and a negative view of former President Donald Trump, but Hispanic men are more divided on both candidates, according to a recent poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US House control teeters on the unlikely battleground of heavily Democratic California
item.description: California's reputation as a politically liberal state is being tested in a string of U.S. House districts that are expected to play into control of the chamber.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Blood, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Harris calls Trump debate decision a ‘pretty weak move,' praises Native community at Arizona rally
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris returned to the Grand Canyon State for a rally on Thursday, speaking out about Native American governance, Trump's debate decision and Project 2025
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's complaints about '60 Minutes' put a spotlight on editing at the nation's top newsmagazine
item.description: Donald Trump skipped a “60 Minutes” interview this past week, but he's attacking CBS for how it handled its session with opponent Kamala Harris, calling it a “giant fake news scam” and suggesting the network was out to protect her.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah candidates for Mitt Romney's open US Senate seat debate approaches to climate change
item.description: The Republican and Democratic candidates vying for Mitt Romney’s open U.S. Senate seat are aiming to convince Utah voters that they have the better blueprint to tackle climate change and protect the state’s vast natural resources.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Obama urges Black men to show up for Harris as he campaigns in critical Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Barack Obama has given a blistering critique of his White House successor Donald Trump and urged Black men to show up for Kamala Harris as he campaigned in Pittsburgh at the start of a swing-state tour for the Democratic ticket.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris was asked to name a few of Trump's virtues. Here's her response
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris was asked to name three virtues that her opponent in next month's election, Donald Trump, possesses — but was unable to come up with even one.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia has suffered more casualties in Ukraine war than all other conflicts combined since WWII: Pentagon
item.description: Russia has suffered some 600,000 casualties in its war with Ukraine — more than its losses in every conflict since World War II combined, according to U.S. officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota’s ‘uncommitted’ voters go into election unsatisfied over lack of cease-fire, conflicted over Harris
item.description: Minnesota sent the most “uncommitted” delegates to the Democratic National Convention in protest of President Joe Biden’s stance toward Israeli’s military actions in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: House Democrats bet on small and fun events for minority outreach
item.description: The House Democratic campaign arm is increasingly relying on small, community-centered events for its get-out-the-vote efforts among voters of color, looking to increase interest with nonpolitical hooks.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lake blames Gallego for border woes, he vows to protect abortion rights in Arizona Senate debate
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Republican Senate candidate Kari Lake repeatedly reminded viewers Wednesday of her three-decade career on Arizona television, looking to harness the intimate connection she built with voters and overcome Democrat Ruben Gallego's portrayal of her as a liar in the only debate of the clo
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER and GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Sen. Tim Johnson, the last Democrat to hold statewide office in South Dakota, dies at 77
item.description: Former Sen. Tim Johnson, a centrist who was the last Democrat to hold statewide office in South Dakota and who spent nearly three decades in Congress, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Some Minneapolis Metro Transit trains will switch to buses starting Oct. 9
item.description: Part of the Metro Transit’s Blue and Green Lines will be closed starting at 10 p.m. on Wednesday for scheduled maintenance. It will reopen by 3 a.m. Monday, Oct. 14.
item.dc:creator: Anika Besst, MPR News

item.title: Michigan US Senate debate: Rogers, Slotkin race could determine control
item.description: Rogers and Slotkin meet on Tuesday for an hour-long debate hosted by Nexstar's WOOD-TV, beginning at 7 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Van Gilder, NewsNation

item.title: Trump campaign blasts Bob Woodward; denies secret Putin calls
item.description: Donald Trump's campaign is rejecting claims in Bob Woodward's new book 'War' about the former president holding secret calls with Vladimir Putin since leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis pushes back on 'delusional' Harris remark about Hurricane Milton calls
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) said Monday that it was “delusional” for Vice President Kamala Harris to accuse him of “political gamesmanship” after reports emerged that he did not take the Democratic presidential nominee’s calls as his state braces for Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Business groups push back after Walz says they supported paid leave program
item.description: While some small business owners advocated for a government-managed leave proposal, the state’s largest business groups sought to block the proposal in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: U.S. Treasury says tribally owned entities should be tax-exempt
item.description: Proposed new guidelines would clarify the decades-old question of whether tribal businesses or entities are exempt from federal taxes.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Harris leads Trump among California Latino voters by 23 points
item.description: Vice President Harris holds a strong lead over former President Trump among California Hispanics, according to a Telemundo poll released Tuesday. The California survey contrasts with a similar poll released earlier this month of Florida Hispanic voters, which found Trump leading Harris.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, Fox 5

item.title: Harris reveals owning a Glock: 'Of course' I've fired it
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris said in a "60 Minutes" interview that aired Monday that she owns a Glock and has fired it at a shooting range in another example of how she and her campaign have made a point to highlight her gun ownership.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Pro-Trump Christian nationalists are on tour to recruit election workers
item.description: “Are you guys ready to show the world that Christians will be silent no more?” said one speaker, whose nonprofit is recruiting people to become election workers.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Hagen and Audrey Nguyen, MPR News

item.title: Fact checking falsehoods about FEMA funding and Hurricane Helene
item.description: Rumors, misinformation and lies about the federal government’s response to Hurricane Helene have run rampant since the storm made landfall, especially around FEMA funding.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Major North Carolina newspaper knocks Trump over Helene response 'falsehoods'
item.description: A leading newspaper in North Carolina is blasting former President Donald Trump over what it calls his "falsehoods" about the government response to Hurricane Helene, which devastated large swaths of the state last week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear arguments in Texas emergency abortion case
item.description: The Supreme Court decided not to hear arguments in a case involving Texas that could have provided an answer about whether a state abortion ban conflicts with a federal emergency care law.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Thousands join pro-Palestinian rallies around the globe as Oct. 7 anniversary nears
item.description: Police used tear gas and water cannons to disperse violent demonstrators in Rome as tens of thousands pro-Palestinian protesters took to the streets in European cities and around the globe, calling for a cease-fire as the first anniversary of the Hamas attacks on Israel approached.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump returns to site of attempted assassination, honors shooting victims
item.description: Former President Donald Trump is returning Saturday to the site of his attempted assassination with a rally in Butler County.
item.dc:creator: Patty Coller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris, Trump campaigns send surrogates to Las Vegas to court middle class, Latinos
item.description: With a month until Election Day, Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Donald Trump sent their surrogates to Las Vegas this week to campaign for Nevada’s six electoral votes.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
